commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=e9423f8124e5c1bae7ac60f63ea4845c8dd66bb4)
commands.review_summary:
The PR recomputes known-priced recorded-zero session costs, clears stale cost breakdowns for summaries and session logs, bumps the usage-cost cache version, and hydrates all-zero DeepSeek configured costs from catalog pricing.

PR surface: Source +37, Tests +184. Total +221 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level reproduction: current main preserves a recorded cost.total of 0 for nonzero usage when flat model pricing is known, and session logs also accept that recorded zero directly. I did not run a live DeepSeek API or Control UI session in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainer review is needed to decide or repair the all-zero DeepSeek configured-cost compatibility semantics and to choose the canonical overlapping fix path.

target.title: fix(deepseek): recover V4 zero-cost spend
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #97047.

DeepSeek V4 usage can still surface as zero spend through two related paths:

- session transcripts may contain nonzero token usage with `usage.cost.total: 0`, which should be recomputed when the model has known positive pricing;
- user-configured DeepSeek V4 model rows can carry an all-zero `cost` object, which previously counted as an explicit cost override and blocked hydration from the bundled DeepSeek catalog.

That combination can leave Control UI Spend and session log views reporting known-priced DeepSeek V4 usage as zero.

## Why This Change Was Made

This change keeps the generic session-cost guard from preserving recorded zero totals for known-priced nonzero-token usage, clears stale cost breakdowns so aggregation uses the recomputed estimate, and bumps the usage-cost cache version so older cached zero totals are rebuilt.

It also tightens the DeepSeek provider policy: catalog pricing now hydrates when the configured model has no positive cost values, while preserving explicit positive user cost overrides.

## User Impact

DeepSeek V4 spend summaries, cache rebuilds, and session logs now show estimated cost for known-priced token usage instead of a fabricated zero. DeepSeek V4 model configs with all-zero cost placeholders recover the bundled catalog pricing automatically.

## Review Items Addressed

- Fixed the `check:test-types` issue by using the cache summary API's typed `startMs`/`endMs` parameters.
- Fixed the session-log fixture so the transcript produces a returned log entry.
- Removed the direct `CHANGELOG.md` edit; release-note context now stays in this PR body only.
- Added redacted after-fix behavior proof for the known-priced DeepSeek V4 recorded-zero-cost path.

## Redacted behavior probe

Temporary state-dir probe with redacted transcript content and DeepSeek V4 catalog pricing:

```json
{
  "probe": "redacted DeepSeek V4 recorded-zero cost recompute",
  "inputTokens": 10000,
  "outputTokens": 10000,
  "cacheReadTokens": 10000,
  "recordedCostTotal": 0,
  "catalogCostPerMillion": {
    "input": 0.14,
    "output": 0.28,
    "cacheRead": 0.028,
    "cacheWrite": 0
  },
  "totalTokens": 30000,
  "totalCost": 0.00448,
  "sessionLog": {
    "tokens": 30000,
    "cost": 0.00448
  }
}
```

sessionLog cost 0.00448.

## Current head key Actions

- Recompute recorded-zero session transcript costs when the model has positive configured/catalog pricing and nonzero tokens.
- Clear stale zero breakdowns so spend summary and session log cost both use the recomputed estimate.
- Bump the usage-cost cache semantic version from 4 to 5 so old zero-preserving cache entries are rebuilt.
- Hydrate all-zero DeepSeek V4 configured costs from bundled catalog pricing while preserving explicit positive overrides.
- Keep `CHANGELOG.md` unchanged relative to `upstream/main`.

## Evidence

- `node --import tsx` redacted behavior probe: `"totalCost": 0.00448`; sessionLog cost 0.00448.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/session-cost-usage.test.ts extensions/deepseek/provider-policy-api.test.ts` passed locally on the PR branch.
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD` passed locally on the PR branch.
- `CHANGELOG.md` has no diff relative to `upstream/main`.
- Added fixture coverage in `src/infra/session-cost-usage.test.ts` for known-priced DeepSeek V4 recorded zero cost, including summary totals, cache upgrade behavior, and `loadSessionLogs()` cost display.
- Added fixture coverage in `extensions/deepseek/provider-policy-api.test.ts` for all-zero DeepSeek V4 cost hydration from catalog pricing.
